traditions: Śākta-Tantra (Mahāvidyā / Dasa-Mahāvidyā); Hindu distinct from Tibetan Buddhist Tārā

documents.title: Agni Māraṇa Tantra
documents.description: A Tantra-text compilation centered on the Agni Māraṇa rite, the use of consecrated fire as the agent of an energetic cessation ritual. The text documents mantra, yantra, and procedural detail alongside cautionary framing. Preserved here as heritage and source material, not as practical instruction.

documents.title: Nimbu Mantra Siddhi Sādhana Manual
documents.description: A short operational manual on the Nimbu (lemon) mantra-siddhi sādhana, a folk-tantric purification rite using a lemon as the ritual substrate. Documented heritage; outcomes are not guaranteed.

documents.title: Sādhanās and Kriyās Collected
documents.description: A compendium of multiple sādhanās and kriyās drawn from tantric, yogic, and devotional sources. Includes foundation practices such as pranava-japa and nāḍī śuddhi as well as specialised kriyās.

documents.title: Vīrudha-Āhāra Māraṇa — The Energetic Food Poison Curse
documents.file: tantra/Vīrudha-Āhāra Māraṇa – The Energetic Food Poison Curse.pdf
documents.description: A specialised māraṇa text detailing the Vīrudha-Āhāra rite, an adversarial tantric procedure framed around energetic contamination of food. Included for scholarly and source-text preservation only. Not instructional; explicit caution flag set.

documents.title: 5 Rare Breath Magic Techniques
documents.description: A short operational manual of five lesser-known breath techniques: Kumbhaka of Silence, Whisper Breath, Spine Flame Breath, the Shaman's Triangle, and the Quantum Pause. Drawn from prāṇāyāma, tantric, and syncretic contemplative sources.

documents.title: Preta-Siddhi — Field Manual
documents.description: A complete field manual for preta-saṁvāda (spirit communication) drawn from Aghora and Kaula tantric sources. Includes preparatory bhūta-śuddhi rites, the Preta-Sandhi Yantra with geometric proportions, the Chakra-Maṇḍala layout, the seven diagnostic Prashna sequence, and the Preta-Mukti Kriyā liberation rite. Preserved as scholarly source material only.

documents.title: Tantra Vidya
documents.description: A comprehensive compendium on Tantra Vidya — the knowledge system of tantric practice. Covers mantra, yantra, ritual procedure, deity worship, and the broader tantric framework. Preserved as heritage / source material.

documents.title: Mantra-Tantra
documents.description: A text on Mantra-Tantra — the science of sacred sound within the tantric framework. Covers the theory and practice of mantra, including bīja-syllables, japa methodology, and the relationship between sound and consciousness.

documents.title: Stambhana Māraṇa — The Freezing Death Ritual
documents.file: tantra/Stambhana Māraṇa – The Freezing Death Ritual.pdf
documents.description: A specialized text detailing the Stambhana Māraṇa rite — a combined immobilization-and-transformation ritual that merges two of the six ṣaṭ-karma (stambhana and māraṇa) into a single operational procedure. HIGH CAUTION — preserved for scholarly study only. Not instructional.

documents.title: Bagalāmukhī Sādhana
documents.description: The eighth of the Daśa Mahāvidyās, Bagalāmukhī embodies *stambhana* — the power to paralyse, silence, or immobilise. High caution — an advanced Śākta Mahāvidyā rite traditionally associated with the arrest of hostile force and of inauspicious speech. Preserved here as heritage / source material. Not instructional.

documents.title: Bālā Tripurasundarī Sādhana (Bālā Pañcadaśākṣarī)
documents.description: The entry-level upāsanā of the Śrī Vidyā tradition, addressed to Bālā Tripurasundarī ("the nine-year-old form" of Lalitā) using the fifteen-syllable *pañcadaśākṣarī* mantra in its Bālā-form. High caution — a Śrī Vidyā dīkṣā-gated mantra; the entry-point of the broader Śrī Vidyā curriculum but nonetheless a vidyā of the Śākta-Mahāvidyā class. Preserved here as heritage / source material. Not instru

documents.title: Bhairavī (Proper) Sādhana
documents.subcategory: Mahāvidyā (fifth) — the teacher-aspect of the Goddess
documents.description: The fifth of the Daśa Mahāvidyās in the canonical Śākta list, Bhairavī (proper) is the Goddess as the teacher (*guru-rūpiṇī*) — distinct from Tripura-Bhairavī (the sixth Mahāvidyā, the fierce form of Tripurasundarī, covered in *tripura-bhairavi-sadhana.md*). Bhairavī proper is the Goddess of *tapas* (austerity) in her universal cosmic form, holding a *mālā* (rosary) and a *pustaka* (book), seated

documents.title: Bhuvaneśvarī Sādhana
documents.description: The fourth of the Daśa Mahāvidyās, Bhuvaneśvarī ("Mistress of the World") is the Goddess as the sovereign of all existence — she whose body is the *bhuvana* (cosmos). Three-eyed, four-armed, holding the *pāśa* (noose) and the *aṅkuśa* (goad), she is the *māyā-śakti* by which the universe is woven. Attested in the *Śāradā Tilaka*, *Bhuvaneśvarī Tantra*, *Bhuvaneśvarī-Aṣṭakam* of the *Rudrayāmala*,

documents.title: Chinnamastā Sādhana
documents.description: The third of the Daśa Mahāvidyās, Chinnamastā ("she of the severed head") is iconographically depicted as self-decapitated, holding her own severed head while three streams of blood feed her two attendants (Ḍākinī and Varṇinī) and her own mouth. High caution — an advanced Mahāvidyā rite whose iconography encodes a deliberate reversal of the polarity of life and death. Preserved here as heritage /

documents.title: Dakṣiṇa Kālī Sādhana
documents.description: The first of the Daśa Mahāvidyās and the foundational Kālī form, Dakṣiṇa Kālī stands upon the recumbent body of Śiva, four-armed, holding a severed head and a cleaver, garlanded with fifty skulls, her tongue extended. The principal textual witnesses are the *Karpūrādi-Stotra*, *Kālī Tantra*, *Todala Tantra*, and *Mahānirvāṇa Tantra*. High caution — an advanced Mahāvidyā rite of the Śākta-Kālī-Kula

documents.title: Dhūmāvatī Sādhana
documents.subcategory: Mahāvidyā (seventh) — Smoke-clad widow / goddess of inauspiciousness
documents.description: The seventh of the Daśa Mahāvidyās, Dhūmāvatī ("she who is composed of smoke") is iconographically an aged, smoke-complexioned widow riding a horseless chariot bearing a crow banner, holding a winnowing basket. High caution — an antinomian Mahāvidyā rite whose iconography deliberately encodes inauspiciousness (widowhood, hunger, decay, smoke, the crow) as a vehicle of liberating insight. Preserved

documents.title: Guhyakālī Sādhana
documents.subcategory: Śākta-Kālī — Secret / Hidden Kālī (Guhyakālī Khanda of Mahākāla Saṃhitā)
documents.description: Guhyakālī ("the Secret Kālī") is the esoteric form of Kālī documented in the *Guhyakālī Khaṇḍa* of the *Mahākāla Saṃhitā* — a many-faced, many-armed goddess manifesting in forms with one to one hundred faces, the principal *daśavaktra* (ten-faced) form having 27 eyes and 54 arms, including human, animal, and bird faces. High caution — the most secret and elaborate of the Kālī-Krama practices. Pres

documents.title: Kāla Bhairava Aṣṭākṣara Sādhana
documents.description: A Śaiva-Bhairava rite addressed to Kāla Bhairava — the "Time-Bhairava" form of Śiva as lord of death and of Kāśī — using an eight-syllable (*aṣṭākṣara*) mantra attested in the Svacchanda Tantra and accompanied by the Kāla Bhairavāṣṭakam hymn attributed to Ādi Śaṅkarācārya. High caution — a Bhairava rite of the Śaiva Mantramārga, traditionally restricted to dīkṣit practitioners. Preserved here as h

documents.title: Kamalā Sādhana
documents.description: The tenth of the Daśa Mahāvidyās, Kamalā ("She of the Lotus") — also called Kamalātmikā — is the tantric form of Lakṣmī: golden-complexioned, seated on a lotus, bathed by four elephants with their trunks. The benevolent, prosperity-bestowing form among the Mahāvidyās. Attested in the *Kamalā Mahāvidyā* (Goswami Prahlad Giri), *Śākta Praamoda*, *Daśa Mahāvidyā Tantra-Sāra*, and the *Mahābhāgavata P

documents.title: Lakulīśa Pāśupata Sādhana
documents.description: The Pāśupata Śaiva tradition attributed to Lakulīśa (c. 2nd c. CE) — the ascetic-only Śaiva path of the *atimārga*, outside the householder Mantramārga. Attested in the *Pāśupata Sūtras* with the *Pañcārthabhāṣya* of Kauṇḍinya (c. 4th–5th c. CE), and complemented by the *Gaṇakārikā*, the *Ratnaṭīkā*, and the *Mṛgendra Āgama*. The path proceeds through stages of deliberate ascetic transgression, ri

documents.title: Mahāmṛtyuñjaya Mantra Japa
documents.description: The "great death-conquering" mantra (Tryambakam) addressed to Tryambaka ("the three-eyed one," i.e. Śiva-Rudra), attested in the Vedic Saṃhitās. Moderate caution — protective Vedic recitation; traditionally performed under a qualified Vedic pandit for specific remedial purposes. Preserved here as heritage / source material. Not instructional.

documents.title: Māraṇa-karma
documents.description: The sixth and most severe of the six ṣaṭ-karma — the transformation / death (*māraṇa*) rite, the most consequential of the *ugra*-operations, producing the targeted transformation or extinction of a hostile force. High caution — the final member of the "fierce four" (vidveṣaṇa, uccāṭana, māraṇa, plus the optional mohana). Distinct from the specific Agni-Māraṇa and Vīrudha-Āhāra-Māraṇa sub-sādhanas

documents.title: Mātaṅgī Sādhana
documents.subcategory: Mahāvidyā (tenth) — Ucchiṣṭa-Caṇḍālinī / goddess of leftover & transgressive offerings
documents.description: The tenth of the Daśa Mahāvidyās, Mātaṅgī (also "Ucchiṣṭa-Caṇḍālinī", "Rāja-Mātaṅgī", "Śyāmā") is the tantric form of Sarasvatī — a green-dark-complexioned goddess offered leftover food (*ucchiṣṭa*) from stained hands, the goddess of speech, music, and the periphery. High caution — an antinomian Mahāvidyā rite whose core offering frame deliberately violates Brahminical purity codes. Preserved here

documents.title: Matsyendranāth / Nāth Sādhana
documents.description: The Nāth Siddha tradition attributed to Matsyendranāth (c. 9th–10th c.) — the founder of the Kaula-Yoginī lineage that evolved into the Nāth Sampradāya under his disciple Gorakhnāth. Attested in the *Kaulajñānanirṇaya* (attributed to Matsyendranāth himself), the *Matsyendra Saṃhitā*, the *Akulavīra Tantra*, and the *Jñānasaṅkalpa Tantra*. The path of the yogic-astral-body, the Kuṇḍalinī-awakening,

documents.title: Nyāsa Steps for Tantra
documents.description: A practical guide to the steps of nyāsa — the ritual installation of mantras and deities on the body. Covers aṅga-nyāsa, kara-nyāsa, and the broader nyāsa framework used in tantric practice.. Moderate caution. Preserved here as heritage / source material. Not instructional.

documents.title: Personalized Bīja Mantra Guide
documents.description: A guide to personalized bīja (seed) mantras — the selection, application, and contemplative use of single-syllable sound-forms in tantric practice. Covers the major bījas (hrīṁ, klīṁ, hūṁ, aiṁ, srīṁ, krauṁ) and their applications.. Moderate caution. Preserved here as heritage / source material. Not instructional.

documents.title: Pratyaṅgirā Devī Sādhana
documents.description: A fierce protective Śākta rite addressed to Pratyaṅgirā (also Atharvaṇa Bhadrakālī, Narasiṃhī, Siṃhamukhī), invoked to counter hostile mantra-prayogas and "reverse" (*viprīta*) inauspicious force. High caution — advanced Śākta rite associated with the Atharvaṇa layer and with abhicāra-counter (pratyangirāṇa) practice. Preserved here as heritage / source material. Not instructional.

documents.title: Śānti-karma
documents.description: The first of the six ṣaṭ-karma — the pacification (*śānti*) rite for calming hostile forces, neutralising disturbance, and restoring equilibrium. Moderate caution (the only saumya member of the six-act framework). Preserved here as heritage / source material. Not instructional.

documents.title: Ṣoḍaśī / Tripurasundarī (Pañcadaśākṣarī) Sādhana
documents.subcategory: Mahāvidyā (third) — full Śrī Vidyā form of the Goddess
documents.description: The third of the Daśa Mahāvidyās and the principal Goddess-form of Śrī Vidyā, Ṣoḍaśī ("the Sixteen-Year-Old") is the full pañcadaśākṣarī (fifteen-syllable) form of Tripurasundarī / Lalitā — distinct from the bālā-pañcadaśī form covered in *bala-tripurasundari-sadhana.md*. Attested in the *Vāmakeśvara Tantra* (= *Nityāṣoḍaśikārṇava*), the *Bhāvanā Upaniṣad*, the *Paraśurāma Kalpasūtra*, and the *La

documents.title: Smāśāna Bhairavī Sādhana
documents.description: The Śaiva-Śākta cremation-ground Bhairavī — the goddess of the *smasāna* (cremation ground) textual strand attested in the *Brahmayāmala Tantra* (Picumata), the *Jayadrathayāmala*, and the *Śāradā Tilaka*. She is the Bhairavī of the early Śaiva Mantramārga's Vidyāpīṭha — the cremation-ground goddess of the Kāpālika-Bhairava cultic complex. High caution — an antinomian Śaiva-Śākta rite of the crema

documents.title: Stambhana-karma
documents.description: The third of the six ṣaṭ-karma — the immobilisation (*stambhana*) rite for arresting motion, speech, or intent in a target. High caution — an *ugra*-category ṣaṭ-karma that paralyzes a hostile force's capacity to act. Distinct from the Bagalāmukhī Mahāvidyā sādhana (the goddess-form whose signature operation is stambhana); here stambhana is catalogued as the standalone ṣaṭ-karma category. Preserve

documents.title: Sudarśana Chakra Sādhana
documents.description: A Pāñcarātra-Āgamic Śrī Vaiṣṇava rite addressed to Sudarśana — the discus of Viṣṇu, personified as the *āyudhapuruṣa* ("weapon-person") Chakraratin — primarily as a protective (*rakṣā*) and *abhicāra*-counter practice, often paired with Nārasiṃha upāsanā. Moderate caution — a Pāñcarātra practice historically transmitted under Ācārya supervision but more broadly accessible than Mahāvidyā rites. Pre

documents.title: Tārā (Ugra / Bhuvaṇeśvarī) Sādhana
documents.subcategory: Mahāvidyā (second) — Ugra Tārā / Ekajaṭā / Nīlasarasvatī (Hindu Śākta)
documents.tradition: Śākta-Tantra (Mahāvidyā / Dasa-Mahāvidyā); Hindu distinct from Tibetan Buddhist Tārā
documents.description: The second of the Daśa Mahāvidyās, the Hindu Tārā ("she who ferries across") is Ugra-Tārā — dark-blue or black-complexioned, standing on a corpse (*śava*), adorned with a garland of severed heads, holding a chopper and a skull-cup. Distinct from the Buddhist Green Tārā / White Tārā. High caution — an advanced Mahāvidyā rite of the Śākta cremation-ground tradition. Preserved here as heritage / sour

documents.title: Tripura Bhairavī Sādhana
documents.description: The sixth of the Daśa Mahāvidyās, Tripura Bhairavī is the fierce (*ugra*) form of Tripurasundarī — the goddess of *tapas* (spiritual austerity) and the transformative fire of practice, blending the beauty-consciousness of Sundarī with the purifying fire of Bhairavī. High caution — an advanced Śrī Vidyā Mahāvidyā rite positioned within the Śrī Cakra frame. Preserved here as heritage / source materi

documents.title: Uccāṭana-karma
documents.description: The fifth of the six ṣaṭ-karma — the driving-away (*uccāṭana*) rite for forcing a hostile force to depart from a place, role, or relationship. High caution — the second of the "fierce four" *ugra*-ṣaṭ-karma (vidveṣaṇa, uccāṭana, māraṇa, plus the optional mohana). Preserved here as heritage / source material. Not instructional.

documents.title: Vajravārāhī / Vajrayoginī Sādhana
documents.description: A Buddhist tantric sādhana of the *yoginī-tantra* (highest yoga tantra) class, addressed to Vajravārāhī ("Adamantine Sow") / Vajrayoginī ("Adamantine Yoginī"), the ḍākinī of the Cakrasaṃvara cycle. High caution — a Buddhist Anuttarayoga-tantra practice requiring highest-tantra empowerment (*abhiṣeka*). Preserved here as heritage / source material. Not instructional.

documents.title: Vaśīkaraṇa-karma
documents.description: The second of the six ṣaṭ-karma — the subjugation (*vaśīkaraṇa*) rite for bringing a person, force, or circumstance under the practitioner's influence. High caution — an *ugra*-category ṣaṭ-karma that acts on another's will. Preserved here as heritage / source material. Not instructional.

documents.title: Vidveṣaṇa-karma
documents.description: The fourth of the six ṣaṭ-karma — the discord-generation (*vidveṣaṇa*) rite for creating enmity, division, or separation between parties (typically hostile parties, or parties allied against the practitioner). High caution — the first of the "fierce four" ṣaṭ-karma (vidveṣaṇa, uccāṭana, māraṇa, plus the optional mohana). Preserved here as heritage / source material. Not instructional.
